markdown.markdown:
::: {.page-header} ::: {.header-text} ::: {.header-title} PROJECTS ::: ::: {.header-subtitle} Research areas and ongoing lines of work in the CALM Lab. ::: ::: ::: ::: hero-intro The CALM Lab’s research is organized around three interconnected areas that together aim to understand attention across contexts of life and mind. ::: :::::::::::: {.grid-3 .section-shell} ::::: {.card .card-interactive .quick-card .is-blue} How do differences in the ability to control attention relate to cognition and well-being? [View Project →]{.link-cta} ::::: ::::: {.card .card-interactive .quick-card .is-yellow} ### Neural Mechanisms What are the neural mechanisms underlying the ability to control attention? [View Project →]{.link-cta} ::::: ::::: {.card .card-interactive .quick-card .is-green} ### Mindfulness How can mindfulness and contemplative practices strengthen attention control and well-being? [View Project →]{.link-cta} ::::: :::::::::::: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ # Individual Differences and Cognitive Mechanisms {#cognitive-section style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-primary);"} ::: {style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-secondary); font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 700;"} How do differences in the ability to control attention relate to cognition and well-being? ::: ::: {.profile-section-card} People differ in their ability to control attention, and these differences are a fundamental aspect of human cognition. We investigate how individual differences in attention control play a role in shaping our perception, memory, reasoning, and well-being. Critical to understanding the role of attention control in cognition and well-being is the ability to measure it. We investigate how to best measure attention control, including the development of new tasks and approaches to measuring attention control in real-world contexts. [View and Download Attention Tasks →](){.link-cta} ::: ### Publications
::: {#cognitive-pubs} ::: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ # Neural Mechanisms {#brain-section style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-primary);"} ::: {style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-secondary); font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 700;"} What are the neural mechanisms underlying the ability to control attention? ::: ::: {.profile-section-card} We investigate the neural mechanisms underlying attention control, including the role of large-scale brain networks and their interactions in supporting attention control across contexts of life and mind. ::: ### Publications
::: {#brain-pubs} ::: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ # Mindfulness and Contemplative Practices {#mindfulness-section style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-primary);"} ::: {style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-secondary); font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 700;"} How can mindfulness and contemplative practices strengthen attention control and well-being? ::: ::: {.profile-section-card} Mindfulness and related contemplative practices have been shown to influence attention, cognitive performance, and resilience. We investigate how these practices can strengthen attention control and promote well-being. ::: ### Publications
::: {#mindfulness-pubs} :::
